name: Hungary (Kádár arms, 1957–1989)

colors.symbolism: the sky in the coat of arms
the wheat wreath and rays in the coat of arms

description: Horizontal triband of red, white and green, charged in the centre of the white band with the Kádár-era coat of arms — a wreath of wheat encircling a red five-pointed star above a small red-white-green tricolour shield on a blue ground, with rays of the sun behind and a red-white-green ribbon at its base.
history: Decreed 23 May 1957, replacing both the Rákosi arms (1949–1956) and the briefly-reinstated historical Kossuth arms of the 1956 revolutionary period. Designed as a less overtly Stalinist emblem under János Kádár — the hammer-and-wheat charge is dropped, but the red star and wheat wreath remain. Used on the national flag until 23 October 1989, when the new constitution removed all socialist symbols and restored the plain red-white-green tricolour.

features.symbolism: socialist emblem — red star above a small Hungarian tricolour shield within a wheat wreath with red-white-green ribbon
